summary: A brief promotional post for an interview with Ian Goodfellow, highlighting his primary piece of advice for breaking into the AI industry: writing and sharing valuable code publicly.

principles.statement: To get noticed in the AI field, write good code and share it publicly.
principles.rationale: Researchers at top labs are actively looking for solutions to specific problems. If you build an interesting project that solves one of these problems and host it on GitHub, the labs will naturally find you.
principles.source_quote: One way that you could get a lot of attention is to write good code and put it on Github. If you have an interesting project that solves a problem that someone working at top lab wanted to solve, once they find your github repository, they’ll find you.

heuristics: Write good code and put it on Github to get noticed by top AI labs.

signature_quotes.text: One way that you could get a lot of attention is to write good code and put it on Github. If you have an interesting project that solves a problem that someone working at top lab wanted to solve, once they find your github repository, they’ll find you.
signature_quotes.context: Advice for getting into AI and getting noticed by top research labs.
